citations.narrative:
The facility is not using APHIS Form 7006 (Record of Disposition of Dogs and Cats) to document the sale of the kittens.
APHIS Form 7006 must be used in order to ensure that all required disposition information is documented and available
for inspection.
Each dealer shall use APHIS Form 7006 (Record of Disposition of Dogs and Cats) to make, keep, and maintain the
required disposition information. The licensee must use APHIS Form 7006 to record the disposition (sale, donation, or
trade) of all adult cats and kittens and ensure these records are available for inspection.

There are 4 bags of unopened cat food stored on the floor of the housing facility. Bags of food stored directly on the floor
could become spoiled or contaminated should they come into contact with spilled water. Bags stored on the floor can also
interfere with cleaning of the facility which could attract pests such as insects and rodents.
Unopened bags of food must be stored off the floor and away from walls to allow for cleaning and to protect the food from
contamination and spoilage. The licensee must store the 4 bags of food off the floor and ensure that all unopened bags of
food are stored compliantly at all times.
At least 1 open bag of cat food was found in the housing facility, and one open bag of cat food was found in the kitten
room. Open bags of food could become contaminated by pests such as insects or rodents.
Open bags of food must be stored in a leakproof container with a tightly fitting lid to protect the food from contamination.
The licensee must place these bags of open food into containers with tightly fitting lids and ensure all open bags of food
are stored compliantly at all times.

One enclosure, containing 1 adult cat and 5 kittens, does not have an elevated resting surface. Failure to provide an
elevated resting surface does not allow a cat to exhibit its normal behaviors of jumping and/or climbing and limits the
space available for the cats to move away from each other.
Each primary enclosure housing cats must contain an elevated resting surface. The licensee must add an elevated
resting surface to the enclosure and ensure each enclosure contains a compliant elevated resting surface(s) at all times.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.75(a)(2)  Repeat
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
The facility is not using APHIS Form 7006 (Record of Disposition of Dogs and Cats) to document the sale of the kittens. 
APHIS Form 7006 must be used in order to ensure that all required disposition information is documented and available 
for inspection.  
 
Each dealer shall use APHIS Form 7006 (Record of Disposition of Dogs and Cats) to make, keep, and maintain the 
required disposition information. The licensee must use APHIS Form 7006 to record the disposition (sale, donation, or 
trade) of all adult cats and kittens and ensure these records are available for inspection. 
3.1(e)  Repeat
   
Housing facilities, general. 
There are 4 bags of unopened cat food stored on the floor of the housing facility. Bags of food stored directly on the floor 
could become spoiled or contaminated should they come into contact with spilled water. Bags stored on the floor can also 
interfere with cleaning of the facility which could attract pests such as insects and rodents. 
 
Unopened bags of food must be stored off the floor and away from walls to allow for cleaning and to protect the food from 
contamination and spoilage. The licensee must store the 4 bags of food off the floor and ensure that all unopened bags of 
food are stored compliantly at all times. 
 
At least 1 open bag of cat food was found in the housing facility, and one open bag of cat food was found in the kitten 
room. Open bags of food could become contaminated by pests such as insects or rodents. 
 
Open bags of food must be stored in a leakproof container with a tightly fitting lid to protect the food from contamination. 
The licensee must place these bags of open food into containers with tightly fitting lids and ensure all open bags of food 
are stored compliantly at all times. 
3.6(b)(4)  Repeat
   
Primary enclosures.

One enclosure, containing 1 adult cat and 5 kittens, does not have an elevated resting surface. Failure to provide an 
elevated resting surface does not allow a cat to exhibit its normal behaviors of jumping and/or climbing and limits the 
space available for the cats to move away from each other. 
 
Each primary enclosure housing cats must contain an elevated resting surface. The licensee must add an elevated 
resting surface to the enclosure and ensure each enclosure contains a compliant elevated resting surface(s) at all times. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
